title: DB migrations — access_grants, credit_transactions, processed_webhook_events
acceptance_criteria: 003_payment_tables.sql creates the 'access_grants' table with columns: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), razorpay_order_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, razorpay_payment_id TEXT, grant_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (grant_type IN ('monthly_pass', 'credits_pack')), days_granted INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (days_granted > 0), expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending','paid','active','expired')), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
003_payment_tables.sql creates the 'processed_webhook_events' table with columns: razorpay_event_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()

004_credit_system.sql creates the 'credit_transactions' table with columns: id UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), razorpay_order_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES access_grants(razorpay_order_id), credits_delta INTEGER NOT NULL, feature TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(). Index on razorpay_order_id.
004_credit_system.sql creates a view or function 'credit_balance' that returns the sum of credits_delta for all rows (positive = purchased, negative = consumed). Must be query-efficient (index used).
Both migration files are idempotent — running bun run migrate twice does not error (use IF NOT EXISTS patterns and schema_migrations deduplication from the existing runner)
src/db/schema.ts exports TypeScript interfaces: AccessGrant, CreditTransaction, ProcessedWebhookEvent matching the columns above exactly (no optional fields where NOT NULL is set in SQL)
No ORM — schema.ts is type definitions only, no query logic
output_format: SQL files plus schema.ts additions, plus a plain-English note explaining why each table exists and any non-obvious constraint decisions
